exhibitors.name: 1Spatial by VertiGIS
exhibitors.description: 1Spatial is a software solutions provider and global leader in managing geospatial data. For more information visit www.1spatial.com.

exhibitors.name: 3-GIS
exhibitors.description: 3-GIS network management solutions connect data, workflows, and teams across telecom and utility networks.

exhibitors.name: Acca Software
exhibitors.description: ACCA Software offers BIM and GIS solutions for AEC industries, including Esri-compatible apps; all software IFC certified, openBIM® compliant.

exhibitors.name: AEC
exhibitors.description: Meet Esri's team of solutions engineers and product experts dedicated to the Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AEC) industry. Discover how ArcGIS enhances planning, design, environmental consulting, construction management, project delivery, and operations workflows. Explore Esri solutions for digital twins, BIM and CAD integration, project collaboration, and more. Visit the AEC Showcase area and become an expert on how Esri helps you design, engineer, and build with location in mind.

exhibitors.name: AI
exhibitors.description: ArcGIS is being enhanced with AI. Meet the team shaping how geospatial intelligence powers agentic ecosystems through MCP, and discover how to put geospatial agents to work across ArcGIS. Experience how embedded AI assistants boost productivity and in‑app efficiency, helping you work faster and smarter. Finally, dive into GeoAI tools and models, including pretrained models, foundation models, and new embeddings analysis tools that drive feature extraction, prediction, and forecasting from multimodal data.

exhibitors.name: Airbus
exhibitors.description: Airbus, is an industry leader in remote sensing and imagery acquisition that provides easy access to multi-sensor, multi-resolution data

exhibitors.name: Airspace Link
exhibitors.description: Airspace Link is an FAA-Approved Drone (UAS) Service Supplier, leading the way in preparing state & local governments for the emerging drone industry.

exhibitors.name: Altitude by Geotab
exhibitors.description: Altitude by Geotab, a software platform that provides reliable and contextualized mobility insights for public and commercial sector decision-makers.

exhibitors.name: America's Geospatial Story: 250 Years in the Making
exhibitors.description: From the Public Land Survey System that mapped a young nation to the satellites and sensors that map it today, America's growth has always been a story told through maps. This special exhibit traces 250 years of American geography and innovation—westward expansion, the rise of aerial photography, the GPS revolution, and the modern GIS platforms shaping our future. See how surveyors, cartographers, and technologists built the spatial infrastructure of a nation. Explore historic maps alongside cutting-edge visualizations celebrating America's semi quincentennial and the geospatial community's role in understanding it.

exhibitors.name: American Association of Geographers
exhibitors.description: We provide geographers with the resources to enter the field, develop their careers, and form professional relationships that last a lifetime.

exhibitors.name: Analytics
exhibitors.description: Spatial analytics gives geographic context to data for greater understanding. No matter where your data lives, in the cloud or a database, ArcGIS enriches your data with location to better understand patterns, trends, and relationships. Our spatial analytics teams will show you how to start with data engineering, geoprocessing tools, modeling, charts, Python, network analysis, raster analysis, and help you evolve to more advanced analysis like knowledge graphs, real-time, big data, machine learning (ML), and spatial statistics. Visit us at the expo to get answers to all your spatial analytics and data science questions.

exhibitors.name: Applied Information
exhibitors.description: Applied Information’s Glance EVP gives emergency vehicles reliable signal preemption using redundant comms and high-quality travel time data.

exhibitors.name: Aquanuity
exhibitors.description: The ONLY fully ArcGIS Pro 3.x integrated hydraulic modeling software for water, sewer, stormwater, and pipeline renewal prioritization. Come see us!!

exhibitors.name: ArcGIS Apps & App Builders
exhibitors.description: ArcGIS apps deliver configurable experiences supporting workflows and understanding of markets and communities. Create custom expressions for dynamic visualizations, integrate with Microsoft's enterprise analytics platforms, and automate data workflows. Apps enable data collection, editing, analysis, and compelling storytelling. Mobile workforces achieve field efficiency while staying synced with the office. Visit the Showcase to meet Esri app experts.
booths: ArcGIS Maps for Adobe Creative Cloud

exhibitors.name: ArcGIS Data
exhibitors.description: ArcGIS Data enhances your decision-making and simplifies your work with a collection of ArcGIS-ready data and data-driven capabilities, like geocoding, routing, and data enrichment, to power mission critical applications. Visit us at the showcase to ask questions and talk with subject matter experts.

exhibitors.name: ArcGIS Enterprise
exhibitors.description:
ArcGIS Enterprise is self-hosted geographic information system (GIS) software for mapping, analytics, and data management. Deploy in your infrastructure, whether on-premises or in the public or private cloud, to meet compliance, security, and customization needs. Take advantage of a variety of deployment options, including cloud-native architectures. Connect to existing third-party data sources, access ready-to-use spatial content to kick-start your workflows, and seamlessly integrate with ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Online, and Esri’s full suite of applications. Whether you’re administering a deployment, supporting users, or planning what’s next, ArcGIS Enterprise helps you meet today’s needs and prepare for the future. Stop by the ArcGIS Enterprise area to: 


	Get answers to your questions 
	See demonstrations of the latest features 
	Share how your organization leverages ArcGIS Enterprise 
	Contribute ideas for future enhancements
booths: ArcGIS Enterprise on Linux (one demo station; two machines)
ArcGIS Enterprise on Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure

exhibitors.name: ArcGIS Living Atlas
exhibitors.description: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World is the premier repository of digital geospatial data and content, curated from the collective work of the entire GIS community. It includes ready-to-use apps, maps, tools, 3D scenes, and data layers to support your GIS workflows. Visit us at the showcase to ask questions, talk with subject matter experts, or see demos of the latest content. Esri curators are available to discuss how you can nominate your own content, making your information more accessible to GIS communities worldwide.

exhibitors.name: ArcGIS Online
exhibitors.description: ArcGIS Online is a complete software as a service for your mapping and analysis workflows that scales with you as you grow to handle significant demand. Let Esri handle software updates and maintenance of your GIS for you so you can focus on advancing your organization’s mission. Visit our showcase to meet the experts, get your questions answered, demo the latest capabilities, provide feedback, and learn more about ArcGIS Online.

exhibitors.name: ArcGIS Pro
exhibitors.description:
ArcGIS Pro is the trusted desktop GIS software, central to the ArcGIS geospatial platform, used globally to make the maps that run the world.
Visit the ArcGIS Pro area to meet the team, explore new features, ask questions, and share feedback. ArcGIS Data Reviewer experts will also be available to help.
 
For questions about 3D editing in ArcGIS Pro, head to the Imagery & 3D area. For questions about editing geodatabases or working with attribute tables, visit the ArcGIS Enterprise area.

exhibitors.name: ArcGIS Solutions
exhibitors.description: ArcGIS Solutions are industry-specific configurations of ArcGIS that help you maximize the investment you are making in location-based data and technology. They align with your business needs, leverage your authoritative data, and help you improve operations, gain new insight, and enhance services. Visit the ArcGIS Solutions showcase area, where you can explore solutions for state and local governments, utilities, and public safety agencies. Learn how to deploy a solution that aligns with your needs and even share your ideas for future ArcGIS Solutions.

This showcase is also an opportunity to connect with industry experts, create valuable partnerships, and gain the tools and insights you need to embark on your next planning project.

exhibitors.name: DeepWalk
exhibitors.description: At DeepWalk, we simplify sidewalk maintenance using iPhone LiDAR to deliver fast, GIS-informed inspections and data-driven improvement plans.

exhibitors.name: Defense & Intelligence
exhibitors.description:
ArcGIS derives meaning from data across your enterprise to achieve National Security objectives. ArcGIS leverages AI, advanced analytic methods, and apps to enhance decision making.
Visit the Defense & Intelligence Area to:


	Discover insights by leveraging geospatial AI integrated with advanced analytics
	Integrate National Imagery, graph analytics, and GIS data to enhance situational awareness
	Employ Defense mapping solutions to create authoritative maps and data for National Mappinga across your enterprise to achieve National Security objectives. ArcGIS leverages AI, advanced analytic methods, and enterprise apps to enhance decision making.

exhibitors.name: Dell/NVIDIA
exhibitors.description: We are a global team united under a single purpose: to drive human progress through the power of technology.

exhibitors.name: Deloitte
exhibitors.description: Deloitte helps organizations leverage the power of location to gain critical insights for improving their mission outcomes. Our dedicated team of analysts, software developers, data scientists, and strategists couple innovative data-driven approaches with cutting-edge geospatial solutions to deliver results—where it matters.

exhibitors.name: Design, Build, and Sustain ArcGIS Systems
exhibitors.description:
Discover how ArcGIS serves as an enterprise‑ready geospatial platform that is configurable, interoperable, scalable, and secure by design. Take the next step toward geospatial excellence and learn how to design, build, and sustain systems with ArcGIS that meet your organization's business needs.

Talk to experts about: 


	Geospatial strategy and governance, including aligning GIS initiatives to business objectives and establishing decision‑making, standards, and accountability. 
	Core principles and pillars of the ArcGIS Well‑Architected Framework, such as  security, performance, scalability and integration with other business systems such as ServiceNow, Salesforce, Microsoft, Autodesk, AWS and Adobe.
	Empowering your workforce to foster engagement and maximize adoption. 
	Installation, software authorization, and administering your deployment. 
	Global readiness, including localization, internationalization, and Esri’s commitment to accessibility and inclusivity.

exhibitors.name: Developer Technologies
exhibitors.description: Connect with Esri developers and engineers who build the ArcGIS SDKs and open source mapping projects. Discover a world of possibilities across web, mobile, desktop, game engines, and server platforms. Come see new capabilities including AI and MCP. Our developer area is essential for anyone looking to design, build and deploy custom experiences with location services, or to extend, script, and automate ArcGIS systems. Join us to fully unleash the potential of ArcGIS in your projects.
booths: ArcGIS Maps SDKs for Native Apps
ArcGIS Maps SDKs for Game Engines

exhibitors.description: Step into the Earth Sciences area, where scientific discovery meets real‑world impact. Engage with ArcGIS through hands‑on demos and user stories that show how an open, interoperable analysis ecosystem connects data to insight, insight to action, and science to decisions addressing critical environmental challenges. Meet experts, share ideas, and see how geospatial workflows accelerate discovery, reduce time spent on data wrangling, and help build resilience for the future.

exhibitors.name: EarthDaily

exhibitors.name: Environment
exhibitors.description: The Environment showcase is designed for GIS professionals supporting environmental protection, land and wildlife management, parks and recreation, and climate resilience. Through real‑world user stories and Esri’s latest capabilities and solutions, you’ll discover practical insights and tools tailored to today’s environmental challenges. Whether you’re exploring new approaches to environmental permitting, real‑time wildlife management, parks and recreation planning, or climate resilience, Esri industry experts will be on hand to help you turn ideas into action.

exhibitors.name: Eos Positioning Systems
exhibitors.description: Eos designs award-winning high-accuracy GNSS receivers for ArcGIS apps on all devices. Visit us to explore our newest GNSS solutions for GIS users.

exhibitors.description: Epson is a leader in the Professional Imaging printing market, delivering high-performance solutions for GIS professionals. Visit proimaging.epson.com

exhibitors.name: Equity & Social Justice
exhibitors.description: The Equity and Social Justice showcase supports organizations working to expand economic mobility in their communities. Connect with Esri experts to explore how GIS helps identify barriers, improve access to opportunity, and inform more equitable decisions. No matter what industry you support, you will discover tools that help people thrive and build pathways to lasting mobility.

exhibitors.name: Es2
exhibitors.description: ES2 delivers value to Government, Ports, and AEC organizations by developing GIS & Drone programs, implementing solutions, and collecting data for you

exhibitors.name: Esri
exhibitors.description: This exhibitor is only used for adding technical sessions as sponsored sessions to enable the Request for Sales Follow Up button.

exhibitors.name: Esri Community
exhibitors.description: Discover Esri Community, a free online resource for finding answers to GIS and ArcGIS product questions, sharing enhancement ideas, and collaborating with Esri users around the globe. Stop by our booth for a personalized demo - we look forward to getting you connected!

exhibitors.name: Esri Services
exhibitors.description:
Visit the Esri Services Area to connect with experts who can help you solve your toughest challenges and make a bigger impact with GIS. Ask questions and get advice that will help you turn ideas into solutions that drive sustained growth and innovation.


	Discover flexible training and certification options for you and your team
	Book a 1-1 appointment for guidance on enhancing your geospatial solutions (limited availability)
	Join us for an Insight Talk in the Implementation Strategies Theater

exhibitors.name: Federal User Showcase
exhibitors.description: The Federal User Showcase features key US Government agencies highlighting their important work that impacts the nation. ArcGIS is the nervous system for each enterprise that helps federal agencies connect communities and apply geospatial innovations that responsibly manage and protect land, food, infrastructure, and the people they serve. At the showcase guests will be able to learn about crucial missions from Wildland Fire, Reclamation, National Mapping, Transportation, Land Management, Census, Indian Affairs, Agriculture, Oversight, Policy and more! Meet subject matter experts from Federal Government agencies to discuss how they're utilizing GIS across their enterprise, developing governance, applying AI, creating digital twins, integrating with other enterprise systems, and ensuring data readiness. Find out how GIS is enabling agency staff and leaders to integrate diverse types of data to derive understanding, operationalize solutions, communicate insights, and engage stakeholders and the public.

exhibitors.name: Fireside Chats
exhibitors.description: Join us at 11:30 a.m. each day of the conference to hear from expert geospatial storytellers as they discuss their approach to using cartographic and storytelling principles to create content that informs, inspires, and influences. You'll also have an opportunity to ask your own storytelling questions during each session. Speakers include Dawn Wright, John Nelson, members of Esri’s MapX team, representatives from the National Geographic Society, student StoryMaps competition awardees, and more.

exhibitors.name: Geodesign for a Changing Climate: the World’s Most Important Design Problem
exhibitors.description: The display features Geodesign and OpenPlanMap, a new innovation of the Esri Community Maps program designed to gather planning data from around the world, share it with stakeholders, and assess the collective global impacts of planning. This display also showcases two approaches to addressing the world’s most important design problem: global climate change. Developed by teams from the International Geodesign Collaboration (IGC), these initiatives explore how bottom-up and top-down approaches to planning can inform one another to create more sustainable communities locally and globally.

exhibitors.name: GEODNET
exhibitors.description: GEODNET is a highly reliable, Web3 RTK Network that uses DePIN principles. Providing 100x improvement in position accuracycompared to standalone GPS.

exhibitors.name: GeoGomm
exhibitors.description: For 30+ years, GeoComm empowers public safety with advanced GIS solutions, delivering real-time location intelligence to emergency response teams

exhibitors.name: GEOGRAPH
exhibitors.description: GEOGRAPH helps teams map and manage fiber networks with a trusted GIS platform, backed by experienced people and real human support you can rely on.

exhibitors.name: GeoMarvel LLC
exhibitors.description: GeoMarvel is an award-winning GIS & Esri Gold Partner delivering scalable GIS, cloud, data, & custom app solutions backed by 30+ years of expertise.

exhibitors.name: Geonexus
exhibitors.description: Geonexus provides seamless ArcGIS integration for enterprise systems serving utilities, transportation, and government organizations worldwide.

exhibitors.name: Geospatial Professional Network (formerly URISA)
exhibitors.description: Stop by to learn about membership, GIS-Pro, the GIS Leadership Academy, Vanguard Cabinet, GISCorps, our Mentoring Network, and more!

exhibitors.name: Getac
exhibitors.description: Rugged Laptops, Tablets, and Video purpose-built for Public Safety.

exhibitors.name: GFT
exhibitors.description: GFT helps owners use GIS across the project lifecycle, connecting data, systems, and teams for clearer, more scalable decisions.

exhibitors.name: GIS Certification Institute
exhibitors.description: GISCI is a non-profit that promotes the advancement of proficient GIS professionals through its international GISP® certification program.

exhibitors.name: GIS Day
exhibitors.description: Visit the GIS Day booth, where we invite attendees to learn how they can participate in and lead GIS Day celebrations around the world. Stop by to explore resources, discover ways to engage students, educators, nonprofits, and communities, and see how GIS is being used to inspire geographic literacy and real‑world problem solving. Attendees can connect with the GIS Day team, get ideas for hosting an event, and register to take part in this global celebration of GIS.

exhibitors.name: GIS for Good
exhibitors.description: The GIS for Good Showcase is a place for those using GIS to maximize the impact of our work to support people, planet, prosperity, & peace using the geographic approach. Network and learn from Esri staff that lead the special programs and initiatives designed to help you apply GIS for Good in support of Climate Change, Conservation, Sustainable Development, Humanitarian Action, and Nonprofits. Come join us as we apply GIS for a more sustainable and prosperous future for all.

exhibitors.name: GIS for Good - Climate Action
exhibitors.description: A changing climate is impacting communities and businesses around the world.  Visit us in the GIS for Good showcase to join the conversation and explore data and tools that can help build more resilient communities.  Learn how GIS can assist throughout your climate resilience journey; visualizing climate change indicators, understanding risk exposure, evaluate options to mitigate current and future impacts and inform and engage citizens.   Come to share, learn and be inspired!

exhibitors.name: GIS for Good - Conservation
exhibitors.description: Visit the Conservation Kiosk to explore how geography strengthens conservation work from science to action. Learn how ArcGIS integrates environmental, social, and economic data to reveal the patterns shaping biodiversity, support collaboration across organizations, and empower teams working in the field. Through maps, analysis, and visual storytelling, GIS provides a shared foundation for understanding place, aligning effort, and advancing conservation outcomes.

exhibitors.name: GIS for Good - Education
exhibitors.description: Learn how ArcGIS enhances teaching, learning and research across K-12 and higher education and improves school and campus operations. GIS education equips students with 21st century skills that prepare them for meaningful careers. ArcGIS supports decision-making for maintaining safe and sustainable institutions. Meet the Esri Education team and learn about teaching resources, software options, and other solutions for educators, students, and administrators.

exhibitors.name: GIS for Good - Humanitarian
exhibitors.description: Delivering humanitarian assistance in both sudden onset and protracted crises of today's world requires geographic insight. Humanitarian organizations around the world apply Esri's tools and solutions to analyze risk and vulnerability, plan response operations, mobilize and implement teams efficiently, and monitor and evaluate their effectiveness and sustainability.  Please join us in the GIS For Good Showcase in the UC Expo to find out more about how you can leverage Esri’s solutions and ensure your organization's efforts have maximum humanitarian impact.

exhibitors.name: GIS for Good - Nonprofit
exhibitors.description: Stop by the Nonprofit kiosk and discover how GIS can power smarter, more impactful nonprofit work. You’ll connect with experts who work alongside nonprofits to strengthen fundraising, grantmaking, volunteer management, advocacy, storytelling, and impact reporting. Come see what’s new and discover practical solutions designed to help you advance your mission‑driven work.

exhibitors.name: GIS for Good - Sustainable Development
exhibitors.description: Please join us in the GIS for Good Showcase at the UC Expo to learn how the geographic approach enables organizations to make a positive impact on people, the planet, and prosperity through the collection, analysis, and sharing of SDG data. Visit with Esri’s global development team members to learn how GIS helps UN, national governments, and local authoritative data producers collect, track, report, and ultimately achieve the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.

exhibitors.name: Global Water Cycle: The Movement of Water
exhibitors.description: All life on earth is sustained by the global water cycle—the movement of water from atmosphere to land to oceans and back to the atmosphere. This interactive map gallery exhibit depicts the phenomenon using seven globes, five representing components of the water cycle, and two representing the daily and seasonal impacts of the sun. The exhibit is a companion to the new Esri Press title Geowater: A Geographic Approach to Water Data and Forecasting by David Maidment.

exhibitors.name: Granicus
exhibitors.description: Granicus helps governments connect with residents worldwide using cloud-based communications, websites, meetings, records, and digital services.

exhibitors.name: Harris County Regional Toll Roads - LED Display
exhibitors.description: This unique display shows regional toll roads in the Harris County \ Houston, Texas area by using LED lights. The display uses ESRI's Experience Builder at an iPad kiosk station to interact with the display allowing the user to query different types of toll road information via LED lights. The lights are connected to the actual GIS data through web services. As viewers look at the map, they will notice QR codes on the map. The QR codes will take the viewer to various ESRI applications specific to the how the Harris County Toll Road Authority uses GIS.

exhibitors.name: HDR
exhibitors.description: HDR is a 100% employee-owned professional services firm specializing in architecture, engineering, environmental and construction services.

exhibitors.name: Health & Human Services
exhibitors.description: Stop by the Health & Human Services showcase to meet with health experts and see how GIS supports communities in crisis and recovery. Explore real-time dashboards and coordinated field operations for disaster displacement and homelessness response. Learn how mapping health needs, coverage, and network adequacy improves access and targets services and investments. See how integrated facility, logistics, and network analysis improves planning, continuity, inspections, compliance, and public health communication.

exhibitors.name: HERE
exhibitors.description: HERE is the world’s leading mapping company.

exhibitors.name: Houseal Lavigne
exhibitors.description: Houseal Lavigne is an innovative planning and design firm leveraging GIS to improve and transform communities around the globe.

exhibitors.name: HP
exhibitors.description: HP is fast-forwarding the industry into a new era where workflows, teams, and ideas move seamlessly from physical to digital, from vision to reality.

exhibitors.name: Hubexo
exhibitors.description: Hubexo is the global leader in providing the essential data-driven insights that shape the built environment.  By delivering innovative data, insights, and software solutions to the global construction industry, Hubexo enables its customers to navigate complex challenges, sell more, improve efficiency, and build more sustainably.  ConstructionWire, powered by Hubexo, offers a suite of vertical-specific, planned construction data solutions for lead generation, site selection, market research, and competitive analysis. It provides critical information for the evolution of commercial real estate. Founded in Sweden in 1936 and operating in 25 countries, Hubexo helps customers sell more efficiently and sustainably and leads the future of construction innovation.

exhibitors.name: Hydrosat
exhibitors.description: Hydrosat is a leading thermal intelligence company delivering infrared satellite data and analytics for resource monitoring and risk detection.

exhibitors.name: Hypack, a Xylem Brand
exhibitors.description: HYPACK, a Xylem brand, has delivered trusted hydrographic survey and dredging software with planning and data visualization tools for over 40 years.

exhibitors.name: IALEIA
exhibitors.description: IALEIA represents public & private sector intelligence analysts through advocacy, training, certification, networking, professionalism & research.

exhibitors.name: ICEYE
exhibitors.description: ICEYE provides 24/7 global monitoring with the largest SAR constellation, aiding organizations from all sectors with objective, data-driven decisions.

exhibitors.name: ICF International
exhibitors.description: ICF is a global consulting services company approximately 9,000 full-time and part-time employees, but we are not your typical consultants. At ICF, business analysts and policy specialists work together with digital strategists, data scientists and creatives. We combine unmatched industry expertise with cutting-edge engagement capabilities to help organizations solve their most complex challenges.

exhibitors.name: Imagery & 3D
exhibitors.description: Visit the Imagery & 3D Showcase to explore end‑to‑end workflows for managing, analyzing, and visualizing imagery and 3D data. Experience reality mapping and 3D capabilities that power digital twins, immersive XR experiences, bathymetry and elevation data management, and more confident decision‑making. Learn how geospatial AI accelerates imagery, point cloud, and video workflows, with outputs like meshes and Gaussian splats enabling high‑fidelity, interactive exploration across desktop, web, and extended reality.

exhibitors.name: IMPRO Satellite Image Processing and Telecommunication Inc.
Indoor GIS
exhibitors.description:
Indoor GIS is a floor-aware system for visualizing, analyzing, managing, and navigating indoor spaces. It supports asset and space management, safety, security, and occupant and visitor experiences. Indoor GIS is foundational for operational digital twins: powered by an Indoors Information Model and floor-aware indoor maps created with ArcGIS Indoors, and extended with real-time feeds, imagery, 3D, AI, indoor positioning, field apps, and integration with third party facility systems. This gives owners and operators visibility and control over operations, improving decision-making, reducing costs.

Stop by to view live demos, meet product experts, and learn about the latest features.

exhibitors.name: Info Desk
exhibitors.description: Make the Info Desk your first stop when navigating the Esri Showcase. Located in the center of the hall, the Info Desk provides both a self-service and full-service experience to guide you to the product, capability, industry, or partner in the expo that can best fit your needs.

exhibitors.name: Infor
exhibitors.description: Infor is a global leader in cloud-based, industry-specific enterprise software. Visit www.infor.com.

exhibitors.name: Inspiration Zone
exhibitors.description: Step into the Inspiration Zone to explore special displays from organizations making a difference, including Tompkins Conservation (this year’s keynote speaker), National Geographic Society, Women in GIS, and more. Discover their stories, connect with their missions, and get inspired by the impactful work happening across the geospatial community!

exhibitors.name: International Association of Assessing Officers
exhibitors.description: IAAO serves property assessment professionals worldwide from government jurisdictions & agencies as well as various business & academic communities.

exhibitors.name: International Association of Crime Analysts, Inc
exhibitors.description: The IACA is a 501(c)3 dedicated to providing training, networking and professional opportunities to crime and intelligence analysts around the world.

exhibitors.name: International Association of Fire Chiefs
exhibitors.description: The International Association of Fire Chiefs (IAFC) represents the leadership of firefighters and emergency responders worldwide.

exhibitors.name: iSpatial Techno Solutions
exhibitors.description: iSpatial delivers AI-powered GIS solutions globally, transforming data into insights for smart cities, utilities, and sustainable development.

exhibitors.name: ITpipes
exhibitors.description: ITpipes delivers smart inspection software and AI solutions, helping agencies turn pipeline data into insight for proactive infrastructure management.

exhibitors.name: Juniper Systems
exhibitors.description: Juniper Systems, Inc. is a manufacturer of ultra-rugged tablets, handheld computers, GPS receivers, mapping software, and field computing solutions.

exhibitors.name: Khatib & Alami
exhibitors.description: K&A an international multidisciplinary consultancy comprising architects, engineers, planners, and other specialists. www.khatibalami.com

exhibitors.name: KloudGin
exhibitors.description: KloudGin is the only mobile-first, cloud-native work and asset management solution purpose-built for utilities and essential service providers.

exhibitors.name: Land Records & Cadastre
exhibitors.description: Explore innovative Land Records and Land Administration solutions using ArcGIS. Learn how top organizations improve spatial accuracy in managing land rights and responsibilities with ArcGIS Parcel Fabric, improve property value assessments, and engage with stakeholders. Also learn how ArcGIS can enhance Right-of-Way (ROW) management by mapping land interests, strategically planning infrastructure projects, and improving safety and compliance.

exhibitors.name: Langan
exhibitors.description: Langan provides GIS, survey, & related technology services in supporting state & local governments, theme parks, resorts, & educational organizations.

exhibitors.name: Laser Tech
exhibitors.description: TruPulse® rangefinders are conveniently powerful on their own & pair with popular GIS programs to add remote positioning workflows to your toolkit.

exhibitors.name: Laserfiche
exhibitors.description: Laserfiche is the leading SaaS provider of intelligent content management and business process automation.

exhibitors.name: Learning Zone
exhibitors.description: Discover the Learning Zone’s Hands-On Learning Lab, open Tuesday–Thursday, where you can build new skills and explore GIS tools. Don’t miss the Special Displays, including the inspiring work of Young Scholars award recipients from around the world. Visit to learn, connect, and get inspired by innovative projects and global talent in geospatial science!

exhibitors.name: Lebanese Red Cross
exhibitors.description: Lebanese Red Cross (LRC), founded in 1945, is a volunteer-led humanitarian organization providing emergency relief and support to vulnerable people.

exhibitors.name: Leica Geosystems
exhibitors.description: With more than 200 years of history, Leica Geosystems, part of Hexagon, is the trusted supplier of premium sensors, software and services.

exhibitors.name: LightBox
exhibitors.description: Leading CRE information and technology platform, connecting people, businesses and data to help facilitate faster, easier, more accurate decisions.

exhibitors.name: Locus
exhibitors.description: Locus are data integration and automation specialists, helping organisations master the use of FME and Esri's Data Interoperability Extension.

exhibitors.name: Locusview
exhibitors.description: Locusview’s Digital Construction Management platform enables utilities to automate data capture during construction & streamline the close-out process

exhibitors.name: LumiDB
exhibitors.description: The spatial data engine that makes petabyte-scale 3D point cloud archives instantly queryable and streamable into GIS, CAD, LiDAR or AI tool.

exhibitors.name: Mach9
exhibitors.description: Mach9 builds AI software that automates feature extraction from LiDAR and imagery, delivering CAD and GIS outputs up to 96x faster than manual methods

exhibitors.name: MaintStar
exhibitors.description: MaintStar is the trusted company with legendary customer service & powerful asset, land, and customer request management software suites and modules.

exhibitors.name: Mapping a Living Planet: GIS for Conservation
exhibitors.description: From Audubon's century of bird population science to the Forest Stewardship Council's global standards for responsible forestry, geospatial technology is at the heart of protecting our natural world. This special exhibit highlights how GIS powers habitat mapping, migratory bird tracking, deforestation monitoring, and certified sustainable land management across millions of acres worldwide. Discover how conservation organizations use location intelligence to guide policy, restore ecosystems, and safeguard biodiversity for future generations—proving that where we look shapes how we protect.

exhibitors.name: MaxVision, Rugged Portable Computers LLC
exhibitors.description: MaxVision builds rugged portable workstations, servers, and displays—durable, high-computing, & configurable for mission‑critical operations.

exhibitors.name: MCCI LLC
exhibitors.description: MCCi is how you can finally break free from the manual and repetitive processes that dominate your document management, permitting and licensing.

exhibitors.name: Michael Baker Inc
exhibitors.description: Michael Baker International & DATAMARK Technologies is a leading provider of engineering, consulting, advisory services & public safety GIS.

exhibitors.name: Michael Bauer Research GmbH
exhibitors.description: MB-Research provides high-quality regional market data and digital boundaries with global coverage for your national and international analysis.

exhibitors.name: Microsoft
exhibitors.description: Our mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. Together, Esri ArcGIS and Microsoft Azure deliver advanced spatial and analytic tools to the cloud securely, bringing location intelligence to your business operations.

exhibitors.name: MipMap
exhibitors.description: GTO/MipMap delivers Digital Twin, GIS, Remote Sensing and Geomatic Engineering solutions worldwide.

exhibitors.name: NASA
exhibitors.description: NASA Earth Science operates more than 20 satellites in orbit & sponsors 100s of research programs to put Earth observation data to use for society.

exhibitors.name: National Geographic Society and Museum of Exploration
exhibitors.description: The National Geographic Society is a global nonprofit dedicated to using science, exploration, education, and storytelling to illuminate and protect the wonder of our world. For more than 130 years, the Society has supported explorers, scientists, educators, and storytellers whose work expands understanding of our world. The newly unveiled National Geographic Museum of Exploration immerses visitors in a new era of discovery. Marking a major revitalization of our campus in Washington, D.C., the museum features an unprecedented collection of photographic, interactive and educational exhibits.

exhibitors.name: National Mapping & Statistics
exhibitors.description: Visit the Mapping & Statistics Showcase to connect with leading experts in aviation, maritime, national mapping, official statistics, and geospatial ecosystems (NSDI/SDI). Discover how geospatial authorities use GIS to produce fundamental and foundational information that addresses socioeconomic, security, and environmental challenges in government and society. Experts will cover ArcGIS Topographic Mapping, ArcGIS Maritime, ArcGIS Bathymetry, ArcGIS Aviation Charting, ArcGIS Hub, and more.

exhibitors.name: National Tribal Geographic Information Support Center
exhibitors.description: Tribal GIS, a non-profit organization, provides geospatial assistance to Native American Tribal Governments and Native American organizations.

exhibitors.name: National Trust for Historic Preservation: ROUTE 66 REMAP
exhibitors.description: In celebration of the 2026 centennial of Route 66, the National Trust for Historic Preservation created ROUTE 66 REMAP, a StoryMap collection that explores the evolving and nuanced story of Route 66 through maps and place-based data. Through the power of geospatial tools, we can acknowledge the Route's past, understand where it's changing, and envision its sustainable and thriving future. Explore a sample of these stories throughout this display.

exhibitors.name: Native Nations
exhibitors.description: Gather at the Native Nations Showcase to meet and reconnect with other tribal & indigenous colleagues from the USA and around the world to hear stories, reconnect with each other, find alignment, and move forward with hope for the future.  Discover how tribes foster environmental stewardship, ensure public health & safety, drive economic development,  promote community resilience, and more with ArcGIS. GIS is the common language for this work; OneMAP for reconnecting our lands and communities. Make sure you check out our custom UC 2026 StoryMap to enhance your event experience and ensure you attend all Native Nations focused events!

exhibitors.name: Natural Resources
exhibitors.description: Visit the Natural Resources showcase to see how location drives better decisions across Agriculture, Forestry, Mining, Petroleum, Pipeline, and Renewable Energy. Explore demos, uncover insights with spatial analysis, and connect with experts and peers to discuss real-world challenges, operations, and innovation.

exhibitors.name: Naviam GIS Technologies
exhibitors.description: Naviam is the world’s leading IBM Maximo partner, helping organizations modernize and maximize the value of their assets through our suite of products

exhibitors.name: NCTech
exhibitors.description: NCTech, a leading provider of HD street-level imagery & 3D insights, empowers field teams to assess ground-truth faster, safer, and more accurately.

exhibitors.name: Nearmap
exhibitors.description: Nearmap is a location intelligence company that provides imagery, insights and answers to manage risk, plan infrastructure, and respond to disaster.

exhibitors.name: NEXGEN Asset Management
exhibitors.description: Meet NEXGEN the most technologically advanced enterprise asset management program on the market, integrating future-first CMMS software with AI.

exhibitors.name: Nodeology LLC
exhibitors.description: 360° video & photo widgets for rapid capture and visualization in ArcGIS, accelerating field-to-desk workflows.

exhibitors.name: Novotx an Accela Company
exhibitors.description: Novotx, an Accela company, is a GIS-centric asset and work management platform built on Esri ArcGIS for utilities and local government teams.

exhibitors.name: NSGIC (National States Geographic Information Council)
exhibitors.description: NSGIC advances national geospatial policy and facilitates collaboration in the geospatial community, enabling informed decision-making through maps.

exhibitors.name: NV5
exhibitors.description: NV5 provides geospatial services and software to solve mission critical challenges – from data acquisition to analytics to enterprise GIS solutions.

exhibitors.name: Ookla
exhibitors.description: We help organizations drive better connectivity outcomes with unmatched network insights.

exhibitors.name: Open Platform
exhibitors.description: ArcGIS is an open, interoperable, standards compliant, secure platform. The Open Platform area features four kiosks covering OGC web services, GIS data formats, data interoperability, ETL patterns, metadata management, catalogs, and search. Topics include open-source technologies, data sovereignty, cloud native patterns, open data/SDI/NSDI, low code/no-code approaches, and Esri Geoportal Server. We look forward to visiting with you.

exhibitors.name: OpenGov
exhibitors.description: Proactively manage your assets, simplify work orders, plan for capital improvements, and make the most of your resources with OpenGov's EAM software

exhibitors.name: Pandell
exhibitors.description: A unified platform that enhances land asset life cycle management through integrated land management, GIS analysis, and location intelligence.

exhibitors.name: ParcelQuest
exhibitors.description: ParcelQuest empowers property intelligence through seamlessly integrating authoritative,  county-sourced parcel data into your GIS environment.

exhibitors.name: Partnering with Esri & Startup Zone
exhibitors.description:
Step into the Partnering with Esri Zone at the 2026 Esri User Conference-your hub for innovation, connection, and opportunity within the Esri Partner Network (EPN). 

Explore a dynamic space where our partners take center stage, featuring: 

Find out how you can join the network by becoming an Esri partner or learn more about collaborating with existing Esri partners to enhance your projects. 

Inviting meeting spaces to connect directly with the Esri Partner Team and discover how to maximize your partnership. 

A Partner Spotlight Theater bringing partner-led innovations to life through live demos and real-world success stories. 

A high-energy Startup Zone spotlighting emerging technologies and the next generation of geospatial solutions from Esri Startup Program partners. 

For our Esri partners, this is your opportunity to connect with your Partner Manager, discover available benefits, and explore what’s next for your business. Stay informed on the latest updates to Lead Generation, Selling Programs, Specialties, and reseller opportunities through Partner Referral-all designed to help you grow your business, strengthen relationships with new and existing clients, and align with Esri technology for continued success. 

 Not a partner yet? Learn how to become an official Esri Partner or connect with organizations in the Esri Partner Network (EPN). EPN partners support Esri users and customers with ArcGIS-enabled solutions, implementation services, and industry-specific content. Visit www.esri.com/partners to get started. 

This is where partnerships turn into possibilities.

exhibitors.name: Penn State World Campus
exhibitors.description: Learn about Penn State’s award-winning programs in GIS, spatial data science, remote sensing, programming, and GEOINT. Earn your master’s online.

exhibitors.name: Penn State World Campus
exhibitors.description: Learn about Penn State’s award-winning programs in GIS, spatial data science, remote sensing, programming, and GEOINT. Earn your master’s online.

exhibitors.name: PipeAid
exhibitors.description: PipeAid delivers precise sewer digital twins, faster inspection coding, and standardized assessments — powered by data as a service.

exhibitors.name: Placer.ai
exhibitors.description: Placer.ai is a leading location analytics platform providing deep insights into consumer behavior and market trends to optimize business decisions.

exhibitors.name: Plenary Zone
exhibitors.description: Visit the Plenary Zone to meet organizations featured in the Plenary Session! Discover how the City of Allentown, NextEra Energy, NOAA’s National Weather Service, and Rete Ferroviaria Italiana are making an impact. Learn from their experiences, explore their innovative projects, and see how they’re using geospatial technology to solve real-world challenges.

exhibitors.name: Polimorphic
exhibitors.description: Polimorphic delivers AI-powered government front desk tools that improve service access, streamline workflows, and enhance resident experience.

exhibitors.name: PreCise MRM
exhibitors.description: PreCise integrates GPS/AVL tracking with GIS for mobile asset monitoring. Our web-based app provides real-time data to optimize operations.

exhibitors.name: Pro-West & Associates
exhibitors.description: Pro-West empowers government and industry with award-winning geospatial data, solutions, systems and training that deliver location transformation.

exhibitors.name: Public Safety
exhibitors.description: Esri’s Public Safety team will be available to advise attendees on how to leverage the power of ArcGIS technology to create a safer more resilient community by addressing the challenges facing today’s first responders. The team will be available to discuss the latest templates, applications, and workflows developed in response to emerging trends. OPS Center Theater presentations will illustrate Esri’s vision of how GIS allows agencies to create safer more resilient communities by improving their ability to respond to public safety and national security threats.

exhibitors.name: Public Works
exhibitors.description: Stop by the Public Works Showcase to explore how agencies are building next‑generation public works operations with GIS. Discover real user stories, live demos, and Esri’s latest enterprise capabilities supporting asset management, field and fleet operations, facilities, solid waste, and more. Connect with subject matter experts to learn how your peers are modernizing workflows, improving visibility, and scaling GIS across the organization.

exhibitors.name: Pulsiam
exhibitors.description: SafetyNet One, powered by ArcGIS, provides real-time situational awareness and mission-critical support for public safety agencies.

exhibitors.name: PwC
exhibitors.description: At PwC, we help clients build trust and transform complexity into advantage with our global, tech-forward network of 364,000+ people in 136 countries.

exhibitors.name: QSIT
exhibitors.description: QSIT, a leading GIS application development and consulting firm, is a proud member of Alkan Holding Group

exhibitors.name: Quantum-Systems GmbH
exhibitors.description: Quantum Systems develops advanced eVTOL sUAS, enhancing range and versatility with edge computing and AI for governmental and commercial clients.

exhibitors.name: Quartic Solutions LLC
exhibitors.description: Helping local governments make GIS work in the real world. We keep architecture simple, performance high, and solutions aligned with your mission.

exhibitors.name: Radiodetection
exhibitors.description: Radiodetection develops solutions that support underground utility locating, mapping, and asset management for critical infrastructure.

exhibitors.name: RAMTeCH Software Solutions, Inc.
exhibitors.description: Since 1992, RAMTeCH—an Esri Gold Partner—has been a global leader in providing innovative geospatial and technology services for utilities and telecom organizations. As a Utility Network Specialty Partner, RAMTeCH offers industry-leading solutions for Managed Services, System Implementation, GIS, and Specialized Consulting Services. From leveraging our Esri Award-Winning Utility Network Advantage Program (UNAP) to support preliminary planning to our proprietary applications for Conflation, Data Migration, and Mobile Applications, the RAMTeCH Team is recognized for high-quality performance, exceptional value, and customer-focused operations.   RAMTeCH operates across three core business units: Spatial Enterprise Solutions, Energy Data Management, and Engineering Services. With over 30 years in delivering successful North American utility projects within the Spatial Enterprise Solutions Team, backed by a global team of 3,200+ professionals and ISO 9001:2015 and ISO 27001:2022 certifications, RAMTeCH empowers organizations to enhance operational performance, reduce risk, and confidently adopt and scale the Esri Utility Network for long-term success.

exhibitors.name: Raven Connected
exhibitors.description: Raven improves public agency infrastructure management by turning your existing fleet into virtual inspectors.

exhibitors.name: Regrid
exhibitors.description: Regrid is the leading provider of land parcels and location context data for your maps, apps and spatial analysis.

exhibitors.name: Relax & Recharge Zone
exhibitors.description: Take a break and recharge—literally! This inviting space offers charging stations for your devices, comfortable seating, and engaging activities. Whether you need a quick reset between sessions or a spot to connect with others, the Relax and Recharge Zone is your go-to place to unwind, refresh, and get energized for the rest of your day.

exhibitors.name: ReportAll
exhibitors.description: ReportAll provides continuously updated, best-in-class nationwide parcel data with its Feature Service for ArcGIS® and LandGlide mobile app.

exhibitors.name: Rextag Corp
exhibitors.description: Rextag is the premier GIS data provider for energy infrastructure data in North America & Europe.

exhibitors.name: RINA
exhibitors.description: At RINA, GIS, engineering, and construction experts deliver sustainable asset management solutions in energy, transport, and infrastructure globally.

exhibitors.name: Rio Hondo College
exhibitors.description: Los Angeles County Rio Hondo College’s GIS Program offers online and in-person GIS certificate and degree, taught by experienced professionals

exhibitors.name: RMSI North America
exhibitors.description: As Network Management Specialty, RMSI helps utilities in GN-UN implementation, LiDAR-based conflation and AI/ML-based auto easement mapping solutions.

exhibitors.name: ROK Technologies
exhibitors.description: ROK Technologies helps organizations migrate, optimize, manage, and scale ArcGIS Enterprise in the Cloud.

exhibitors.name: RouteSmart Technologies
exhibitors.description: RouteSmart Technologies provides route optimization solutions that solve the most complex vehicle routing challenges.

exhibitors.name: Safe Software
exhibitors.description: Safe Software helps transform organizations with FME, the only All-Data Any-AI Enterprise Integration Platform with support for spatial data.

exhibitors.name: Samsara
exhibitors.description: The pioneer of the Connected Operations™ Cloud, a platform that helps organizations harness IoT data to gain insights and improve physical operations.

exhibitors.name: San Diego Region GIS: TOGETHER, WE MAP THE REGION
exhibitors.description: This display showcases how public agencies and academic institutions across the San Diego region are leveraging GIS to enhance operations, support decision-making, and better serve their communities. From improving internal workflows for staff to delivering transparent, accessible data to the public, these organizations are transforming how services are planned and delivered. This display highlights collaborative efforts that use ArcGIS to drive innovation, efficiency, and community impact.

exhibitors.name: San Francisco State University
exhibitors.description: School of the Environment and the Institute for Geographic Information Science at San Francisco State University.

exhibitors.name: SBS
exhibitors.description: SBS is the leader in intelligent design solutions for electric & gas distribution networks, transmission networks, substations, and broadband networks

exhibitors.name: Schneider Electric
exhibitors.description: Schneider Electric is a world leader in helping utilities on their digital transformation journey with solutions and services for Grids of the Future.

exhibitors.name: Shovels
exhibitors.description: Shovels uses AI to clean and unify fragmented permit, contractor, and property data—turning public records into Shovel-ready intelligence.

exhibitors.name: Sidwell
exhibitors.description: Sidwell delivers enterprise GIS solutions for government, specializing in land records, parcel management, and ArcGIS implementations.

exhibitors.name: SkyFi
exhibitors.description: Dual-use geospatial intelligence marketplace connecting users to Earth observation data and analytics.

exhibitors.name: SkyWatch
exhibitors.description: SkyWatch's Content Store makes it easy to browse and buy ready-to-use premium satellite imagery for your GIS workflows.

exhibitors.name: Solonic
exhibitors.description: Solonic

exhibitors.name: SparkPNT
exhibitors.description: High-precision positioning, navigation and timing hardware that's powerful, user-upgradeable, and able to be repaired.

exhibitors.name: Spexi Geospatial Inc
exhibitors.description: The Aerial Intelligence Layer That’s Always On, Always Updated

exhibitors.name: SSP Innovations, LLC
exhibitors.description: SSP Innovations helps utilities modernize their GIS operations through expert strategy, Utility Network migration, and managed geospatial services.

exhibitors.name: State and Local Government
exhibitors.description: Reimagined for a better experience, the State and Local Government area gives you more opportunities to connect with industry experts. Visit the dedicated Resource Center to grab free educational resources and the Spotlight theater for featured sessions with subject matter experts and technical staff. Explore the solutions helping governments of all sizes expand GIS across departments and discover how geospatial analysis can strengthen policy development and decision‑making.

exhibitors.name: Storytelling with Maps
exhibitors.description: Explore special displays that celebrate the diverse voices of our users and showcase unique storytelling approaches and formats. Join fellow GIS enthusiasts at cozy “fireside chats” featuring both users and experts as they share insights and experiences. Check the detailed agenda for session times and don’t miss this inspiring area dedicated to the art of storytelling with maps!

exhibitors.name: StreetLight
exhibitors.description: Transportation analytics for efficient, safe, and sustainable infrastructure

exhibitors.name: Sun Ridge Systems, Inc.
exhibitors.description: RIMS by Sun Ridge Systems is a premier, integrated, and trusted CAD/RMS platform powering real-time public safety operations.

exhibitors.name: Symetri
exhibitors.description: Symetri provides ArcGIS Enterprise services, BIM-to-GIS integration, digital twin solutions, and asset lifecycle intelligence for the AECO industry.

exhibitors.name: Technical Support
exhibitors.description: Visit the Technical Support area to get one-on-one help from Support Analysts and Engineers. Get assistance troubleshooting software issues, exploring best practices, and following up on existing cases or reported bugs. Our team provides personalized guidance and keeps you informed on progress, helping you resolve issues efficiently and get the most out of your ArcGIS software.

exhibitors.name: TEKsystems
exhibitors.description: TEKsystems Global Services is a leading provider of business and technology services.

exhibitors.name: TerraGo Technologies
exhibitors.description: TerraGo develops innovative software for the dissemination and collaboration of GEOINT between national systems and field operators for any mission.

exhibitors.name: Texas A&M University
exhibitors.description: The Department of Geography at Texas A&M University provides undergraduate and graduate degrees in Geography and Geographic Information Science.

exhibitors.name: Texas A&M University
exhibitors.description: The Department of Geography at Texas A&M University provides undergraduate and graduate degrees in Geography and Geographic Information Science.

exhibitors.name: The Living Forest
exhibitors.description: “The Living Forest,” showcases how Earth Observation, AI, and GIS make environmental change tangible. It transforms complex geospatial insights into a compelling public experience, strengthening Esri’s brand presence far beyond our traditional audiences.

exhibitors.name: The UCSB Guide to the Geographic Approach
exhibitors.description: The UCSB Guide is a collaborative, open-access initiative designed to address the challenge of keeping pace with rapid technological change by providing educators with a comprehensive series of ready-to-use, problem-based, ethics-embedded learning modules that bridge the gap between academic theory and the practical frontier of modern GIS, Remote Sensing, GeoAI, and Spatial Data Science.

exhibitors.name: Timmons Group, Inc.
exhibitors.description: When clients need clarity and control of fiber and utility systems, Timmons Group delivers Utility Network, strategic planning, and asset management.

exhibitors.name: Tompkins Conservation: Wild Beyond Borders
exhibitors.description: ILD BEYOND BORDERS: A CONTINENTAL VISION takes you behind the scenes of rewilding as we face catastrophic biodiversity loss, asking the question: how do we change the end of the story? President and co-founder of Tompkins Conservation and former CEO of Patagonia, Inc., Kris Tompkins has helped create 16 national parks, while bringing back 35 species. Her shared legacy with her late husband Doug Tompkins continues today through Rewilding Argentina and Rewilding Chile. Their story is told in Wild Life, a documentary by the Oscar-winning directors Jimmy Chin and Chai Vasarhelyi.

exhibitors.name: TomTom
exhibitors.description: Billions of data points. Millions of sources. Thousands of communities. We are the mapmaker bringing it together to build the world’s smartest map.

exhibitors.name: Topcon Positioning Systems
exhibitors.description: Topcon, a leader in precision technology, transforms global infrastructure by leveraging innovative solutions and the power of precision.

exhibitors.name: Transportation
exhibitors.description: The Transportation Showcase will highlight solutions and best practices for roads and highways, ports, airports, public transit, and rail professionals. Stop by to ask our subject matter experts and technical staff about the technologies and capabilities that are helping your peers improve mobility, manage right-of-way, and ensure safe and reliable transportation systems for those you serve.

exhibitors.name: TRC Companies, Inc
exhibitors.description: TRC delivers GIS consulting, implementation and integration for grid operators. Our 750+ experts offer the industry’s deepest grid OT expertise.

exhibitors.name: Trimble Inc.
exhibitors.description: As an Esri Platinum Partner, Trimble is empowering ArcGIS users with the latest solutions in reality capture and asset lifecycle management.

exhibitors.name: Tyler Technologies, Inc.
exhibitors.description: Tyler Technologies is a leading provider of integrated software and technology services for the public sector.

exhibitors.name: U.S. Census Bureau
exhibitors.description: The Census Bureau's mission is to serve as the nation’s leading provider of quality data about its people and economy.

exhibitors.name: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency
exhibitors.description: The U.S Environmental Protection Agency leverages geospatial technology to support its mission of EPA to protect human health and the environment.

exhibitors.name: UDC
exhibitors.description: UDC is a Platinum Esri Partner, offering GIS-centric system integration services to utilities and infrastructure companies globally.

exhibitors.description: UNIGIS is a worldwide network of universities cooperating in the design and delivery of distance learning in GIS and GIScience

exhibitors.name: University of Arizona GIST Program
exhibitors.description: The UArizona (GIST) program provides educational training (BA/MS/Cert degrees) for those seeking employment in the high growing geospatial industry.

exhibitors.name: University of Denver
exhibitors.description: The University of Denver is one of the premier institutions of higher education in geographic information science (GISc).

exhibitors.description: Our MS programs in GIS and GEOINT are dedicated to providing cutting-edge, graduate-level training and education.

exhibitors.name: University of Minnesota
exhibitors.description: Proud of world-class education, groundbreaking research, and our commitment to partnering with communities in our drive to serve Minnesota.

exhibitors.name: University of Redlands GIS Department
exhibitors.description: At University of Redlands, students use GIS, data science, and spatial technologies to solve real-world problems and build workplace competencies.

exhibitors.description: Learn anywhere with us with this flexible 12-credit certificate program. Learn geospatial technology skills from working GIS professionals.

exhibitors.description: UP42 transforms how organizations order, access, and analyze Earth observation data. We bring together the world’s leading providers, in one platform.

exhibitors.name: Urban Sky
exhibitors.description: We deliver low-cost, high-resolution imagery and wildfire monitoring using tactically deployed stratospheric balloons.

exhibitors.name: US Department of Energy Pacific Northwest National Laboratory
exhibitors.description: PNNL focuses on advancing scientific knowledge and technology solutions for energy resilience, national security, and environmental sustainability

exhibitors.name: USC Spatial Sciences Institute
exhibitors.description: USC Spatial Sciences Institute leads interdisciplinary research and education in GIS and spatial data science solutions for local & global challenges

exhibitors.name: USC Spatial Sciences Institute
exhibitors.description: USC Spatial Sciences Institute leads interdisciplinary research and education in GIS and spatial data science solutions for local & global challenges

exhibitors.name: User Research Lab
exhibitors.description:
Making great technology depends on understanding why and how people use it. We invite you to be a part of Esri’s user-centered design process and help us put this approach into action. Share your thoughts and participate in research that will influence the tools that thousands of people use every day.


At the Esri User Research Lab, you can:


	Complete a 60-minute research session for Esri swag (watch your inbox for a sign-up link in January!)
	Participate in short studies that explore or test things we are currently designing
	Give feedback to improve your experience with Esri software, apps, websites, and services
	Sign up to get involved in future testing events and research activities

exhibitors.name: Utilities, Telecom, and Water
exhibitors.description: Connect with our full utilities team and discover solutions designed to address challenges across the Electric, Gas, Telecommunications, Water, Water Resources, and Utility Network sectors. This year, we’ll also feature a new kiosk showcasing Imagery, 3D, and Reality Capture. Explore capabilities in modern network management, mobile workflows, and advanced real-time analytics. As in previous years, meet our Account Managers, Solutions Engineers, Professional Services, and Marketing teams all in one place.

exhibitors.name: Vantor
exhibitors.description: Vantor is forging the new frontier of spatial intelligence to unlock a more autonomous, interoperable world.

exhibitors.name: Verisk Maplecroft
exhibitors.description: Identify, analyse and monitor external business risks and opportunities across operations, supply chains and investments

exhibitors.name: Verizon Connect
exhibitors.description: Verizon Connect Solutions, Fleet Tracking, Fleet Dashcam, Field Service & Scheduling, Compliance Management, Asset Tracking

exhibitors.name: VertiGIS
exhibitors.description: VertiGIS is a leading spatial asset data management solution provider, helping organizations accomplish even more with Esri’s ArcGIS.

exhibitors.name: Vexcel
exhibitors.description: Vexcel Data Program is the largest aerial imagery program in the world, collecting high-res imagery and geospatial data in 40+ countries.

exhibitors.name: ViewPro
exhibitors.description: ViewPro offers innovative use of GIS technology for Cities & Local Government to provide enhanced visibility into their daily workflow.

exhibitors.name: What in the World?
exhibitors.description: Interact with and interpret imagery from the Content Store for ArcGIS to answer questions and you could win a prize!

exhibitors.name: Wingtra
exhibitors.description: Headquartered in Switzerland, Wingtra is the world’s leading VTOL drone producer for mapping, survey and mining industry professionals. Since its market entry in early 2017, Wingtra has partnered with more than 70 of the biggest equipment dealers and has been selling mapping drones globally ever since.

exhibitors.name: Women in GIS (Awards and Scholarships Committee)
exhibitors.description: Women in GIS (WiGIS) is a vibrant community uniting women passionate about geospatial technologies. Visit our special display in the Map Lounge to discover how you can get involved! We're here to break down barriers like job discrimination, pay gaps, and professional isolation, empowering women to thrive in their careers. Through WiGIS, you'll find a supportive network that fosters connections, resource sharing, and professional growth. And, we welcome allies who share our commitment to a more equitable geospatial community. We also celebrate excellence through our Awards & Scholarships Program, recognizing outstanding women with awards and scholarships in partnership with Esri.
booths: Women in GIS (Awards and Scholarships Committee)

exhibitors.name: Woodwell Climate Research Center
exhibitors.description: Woodwell Climate Research Center conducts science for solutions at the nexus of climate, people, and nature.

exhibitors.name: World Geospatial Industry Council
exhibitors.description: WGIC is a global not-for-profit representing private geospatial companies, advancing policy, advocacy, and industry collaboration worldwide.

exhibitors.name: WSP
exhibitors.description: WSP is one of the world’s leading professional services firms, uniting its engineering, advisory and science-based expertise to shape communities to advance humanity. From local beginnings to a globe-spanning presence today, WSP operates in over 50 countries and employs approximately 73,000 professionals, known as Visioneers. Together they pioneer solutions and deliver innovative projects in the transportation, infrastructure, environment, building, energy, water, and mining and metals sectors. WSP is publicly listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX:WSP).

exhibitors.name: Xoople
exhibitors.description: Building the Earth’s System of Record™

exhibitors.name: YMCA of the USA
exhibitors.description: See how the YMCA is putting geoanalytics into practice to drive real community impact. Visit our booth to learn how we’ve combined local and national data into accessible tools, like the Community Insights app, that help YMCA leaders identify unmet needs, uncover opportunity, and make stronger, place-based decisions.

exhibitors.name: Young Professionals Network
exhibitors.description: The Esri Young Professionals Network (YPN) booth is designed to be a central hub for all GIS professionals; especially if you are wanting to make new connections and collaborate with the Esri YPN staff. Plan to swing by the booth in Exhibit Hall A (by the demo theatres) to get resources for career growth, expanding your network, and learn about our new YPN Ambassador Program and YPN Chapters across the United States.

exhibitors.name: Young Scholars
exhibitors.description: Esri is constantly working on developing the next generation of GIS leaders. The Young Scholars program allows Esri, our international distributors, and the international education team to recognize exemplary work of current undergraduate and graduate students majoring in geospatial science disciplines at international universities. Winning entries come from all over the world and are selected by a university panel formed by Esri’s distributor in the recipient’s respective country. The Young Scholar recipients travel to San Diego and get to expose their work at the Map Gallery.
